Lidarr.description: Lidarr is an automatic music collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users that monitors RSS feeds for new albums and manages downloads. It integrates with download clients and media servers to organize and rename music files.
Lidarr.notes: Part of the *arr suite. Requires a download client and indexer. Default web UI port is 8686.

ModemManager.service.description: Modem Manager daemon - provides unified API for communicating with mobile broadband modems, 3G/4G/5G devices, and satellite modems
ModemManager.service.notes: Manages mobile broadband devices and modems. Provides D-Bus API for applications like NetworkManager to control modems.

NetworkManager-wait-online.service.description: NetworkManager wait online service - waits for network connectivity before allowing system startup to continue
NetworkManager-wait-online.service.notes: Ensures system waits for network connectivity during boot. Part of NetworkManager suite for network dependency management.

NetworkManager.service.description: Network Manager daemon - manages network interfaces, connections, WiFi, VPN, and provides automatic network configuration
NetworkManager.service.notes: Modern network management service. Handles WiFi, Ethernet, VPN, mobile broadband connections with automatic configuration and roaming support.

Prowlarr.description: Prowlarr is an indexer manager and proxy for the *arr application stack, supporting both torrent trackers and Usenet indexers. It centralizes indexer management for Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr, and similar applications.
Prowlarr.notes: Replaces Jackett for *arr integrations. Default web UI port is 9696.

Radarr.description: Radarr is an automatic movie collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users that monitors for new movies and manages downloads. It integrates with download clients and media servers to organize and rename movie files.
Radarr.notes: Part of the *arr suite. Default web UI port is 7878.

Readarr.description: Readarr is an ebook and audiobook collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users, similar to Sonarr but for books. Note: the project was officially retired in early 2026 due to metadata source issues.
Readarr.notes: Project is retired/unmaintained as of 2026. Default web UI port is 8787.

Sonarr.description: Sonarr is a PVR (Personal Video Recorder) for newsgroup and BitTorrent users that monitors RSS feeds for new TV show episodes and manages downloads. It integrates with download clients to automatically sort and rename episodes.
Sonarr.notes: Part of the *arr suite. Default web UI port is 8989.

Whisparr.description: Whisparr is an adult video collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users, built on the same *arr stack as Sonarr and Radarr. It monitors RSS feeds and manages downloads of adult content.
Whisparr.notes: Part of the *arr suite. Default web UI port is 6969.

accounts-daemon.service.access: System service - D-Bus interface for user account management
accounts-daemon.service.description: Accounts Service - D-Bus service for querying and manipulating user account information. Provides interfaces for user management operations.
accounts-daemon.service.notes: System service that provides D-Bus interfaces for user account operations. Used by desktop environments and system tools for user management. Can be safely disabled on headless servers.

aced.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP daemon responsible for access control and enforcement. Part of the BIG-IP APM (Access Policy Manager) subsystem.
aced.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Not configurable directly by end users.

acpid.service.description: acpid is a daemon that delivers ACPI (Advanced Configuration and Power Interface) hardware events to user-space programs. It handles power button presses, lid close/open, battery events, and similar hardware signals.
acpid.service.notes: Listens on /proc/acpi/event or a netlink socket. Rules stored in /etc/acpi/events/. Commonly used on laptops and desktops.

admd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP administrative daemon. Handles administrative management functions within the BIG-IP platform.
admd.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Not configurable directly by end users.

alsa-restore.service.description: ALSA sound state restoration service - Restores the sound card state at system startup from saved configuration
alsa-restore.service.notes: One-shot service that restores ALSA sound card mixer settings from /var/lib/alsa/asound.state. Essential for maintaining audio settings across reboots.

apache-htcacheclean.service.description: Apache HTTP cache cleaner - Maintains Apache mod_cache_disk cache size within specified limits
apache-htcacheclean.service.notes: Daemon that monitors and cleans Apache's disk cache to prevent unlimited growth. Runs as www-data user for security.

apache2.description: Apache HTTP Server - World's most widely used web server software
apache2.notes: Popular open-source web server. Typically serves content on ports 80 (HTTP) and 443 (HTTPS).

apache2.service.description: Apache HTTP Server - The world's most widely used web server software providing HTTP and HTTPS services
apache2.service.notes: Main Apache web server service. Serves web content and handles HTTP/HTTPS requests. Critical for web hosting operations.

apmd.description: Internal QNAP NAS daemon for Advanced Power Management. Handles power management functions on QNAP NAS devices.

apparmor.service.description: AppArmor security profiles - Linux Security Module providing mandatory access control to restrict application capabilities
apparmor.service.notes: Critical security service that loads AppArmor profiles to restrict application access. Provides sandboxing and prevents privilege escalation. Should not be disabled.

apport.service.description: Apport crash reporting service - Automatically collects and reports application crashes and system errors for debugging
apport.service.notes: Ubuntu's crash reporting system. Collects crash dumps and system information when applications fail. Can be disabled for privacy.

asm_config_serv.description: Internal QNAP NAS configuration service for the ASM (Advanced Storage Manager) subsystem. Manages storage configuration on QNAP devices.

atd.service.description: atd is the daemon that executes one-time jobs scheduled with the 'at' command. Unlike cron which handles recurring tasks, atd runs jobs at a specific future time and then removes them.
atd.service.notes: Jobs are stored in /var/spool/at. Access controlled via /etc/at.allow and /etc/at.deny.

auditd.service.description: auditd is the userspace component of the Linux Auditing System, responsible for writing audit records to disk. It logs security-relevant events such as file access, system calls, authentication, and privilege escalation.
auditd.service.notes: Configured via /etc/audit/auditd.conf. Rules managed with auditctl. Logs stored in /var/log/audit/audit.log.

autofs.service.description: Autofs automounter service - Automatically mounts and unmounts filesystems on demand, commonly used for NFS and CIFS shares
autofs.service.notes: Automatically mounts network filesystems when accessed and unmounts them when idle. Useful for NFS, CIFS, and removable media.

avahi-daemon.service.access: Network service - Multicast DNS and service discovery
avahi-daemon.service.description: Avahi mDNS/DNS-SD daemon - Zero-configuration networking service for automatic discovery of devices and services on local networks
avahi-daemon.service.notes: Implements Apple Bonjour/Zeroconf protocol for automatic service discovery. Enables .local hostname resolution and service advertising. Used by printers, file shares, and media servers.

avrd.description: Internal QNAP NAS daemon for AVR (Automatic Volume Recognition) functions. Manages volume detection and storage subsystem events on QNAP devices.

bd.description: The BIG-IP 'bd' (blade daemon) is an internal F5 process that handles traffic processing and load-balancing decisions at the data plane level.
bd.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Core traffic handling daemon.

big3d.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP daemon used by GTM/DNS for gathering performance metrics from BIG-IP devices across data centers. Supports Global Traffic Manager health monitoring.
big3d.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Used by BIG-IP DNS (formerly GTM).

blk-availability.service.description: Block device availability service - Ensures proper activation and deactivation of LVM volume groups and device mapper devices
blk-availability.service.notes: One-shot service that activates block devices during boot and properly deactivates them during shutdown. Important for LVM and device mapper setups.

bluetooth.service.description: Bluetooth daemon - Manages Bluetooth adapters and provides Bluetooth connectivity for devices like headphones, keyboards, and mice
bluetooth.service.notes: BlueZ Bluetooth stack daemon. Manages Bluetooth adapters and handles pairing, connections, and protocols. Can be disabled if Bluetooth is not needed.

botd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP bot detection daemon. Part of the BIG-IP Advanced WAF (Web Application Firewall) bot defense functionality.

bthelper@hci0.service.description: A systemd service that initializes and configures Bluetooth hardware controller hci0 at boot. Part of the BlueZ Bluetooth stack on Linux.
bthelper@hci0.service.notes: Template unit instantiated per Bluetooth adapter (hci0, hci1, etc.). Part of the bluez package.

casaos.access: Web service - Personal cloud management interface
casaos.description: CasaOS - Open-source personal cloud system with web-based management interface for self-hosted applications and Docker containers
casaos.notes: Personal cloud operating system that provides a simple web interface for managing Docker-based applications, file sharing, and media services.

casaos-app-management.service.description: CasaOS App Management Service - Manages Docker container applications within the CasaOS personal cloud ecosystem
casaos-app-management.service.notes: Microservice responsible for managing Docker-based applications in CasaOS. Handles app installation, updates, and lifecycle management.

casaos-gateway.service.description: CasaOS Gateway Service - API gateway and routing service for CasaOS microservices architecture
casaos-gateway.service.notes: API gateway that routes requests between CasaOS web interface and backend microservices. Essential for CasaOS functionality.

casaos-local-st.access: Personal cloud service - Local storage management
casaos-local-st.description: CasaOS Local Storage - Component for managing local storage and file operations in the CasaOS personal cloud system
casaos-local-st.notes: Manages local storage operations and file system access for CasaOS applications and user data.

casaos-local-storage.service.access: Personal cloud service - Local storage management
casaos-local-storage.service.description: CasaOS Local Storage Service - Manages local storage, disk operations, and file system access for the CasaOS personal cloud
casaos-local-storage.service.notes: Handles storage management, disk monitoring, and file operations for CasaOS. Provides storage APIs for other CasaOS services.

casaos-message-bus.service.description: CasaOS Message Bus Service - Handles inter-service communication and event messaging between CasaOS microservices
casaos-message-bus.service.notes: Message bus service that enables communication between different CasaOS microservices. Essential for coordinated operations.

casaos-user-ser.description: CasaOS User Service - Manages user accounts, authentication, and authorization within the CasaOS personal cloud system
casaos-user-ser.notes: Handles user authentication, authorization, and user management features for CasaOS web interface and applications.

casaos-user-service.service.description: CasaOS User Service - Provides user authentication, authorization, and account management for CasaOS personal cloud users
casaos-user-service.service.notes: Microservice responsible for user account management, authentication, and access control in CasaOS environment.

casaos.service.access: Personal cloud service - Main CasaOS interface
casaos.service.description: CasaOS Main Service - Open-source personal cloud system providing web-based management for Docker applications, file sharing, and smart home integration
casaos.service.notes: Main CasaOS service providing web interface for personal cloud management. Coordinates with other CasaOS microservices to provide Docker app management, file sharing, and media services.

chmand.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP chassis management daemon. Manages hardware chassis components and inter-blade communication in multi-blade BIG-IP systems.

chrony.service.description: chronyd is an implementation of the Network Time Protocol (NTP) daemon that synchronizes the system clock with NTP servers. It is particularly well-suited for systems with intermittent connectivity or that are frequently suspended.
chrony.service.notes: Configuration file at /etc/chrony/chrony.conf (Debian) or /etc/chrony.conf (RHEL/SUSE). Uses UDP port 123.

cloud-config.service.description: Cloud-config service - Third stage of cloud-init sequence that applies configuration after networking is available, including package installation and system configuration
cloud-config.service.notes: Part of cloud-init system for automated cloud instance configuration. Runs after networking is established to install packages, configure services, and apply user data.

cloud-final.service.description: Cloud-final service - Fourth and final stage of cloud-init that runs user scripts and performs final system configuration tasks
cloud-final.service.notes: Final stage of cloud-init that executes user scripts, phone-home reporting, and final configuration tasks. Runs at rc.local-like priority.

cloud-init-local.service.description: Cloud-init local service - First stage of cloud-init that prepares system before networking starts, including local data source discovery and initial system setup
cloud-init-local.service.notes: Early stage cloud-init service that runs before networking. Identifies data sources, prepares file systems, and configures networking for subsequent stages.

cloud-init.service.description: Cloud-init service - Second stage of cloud-init that configures SSH keys, user accounts, and system settings after local initialization
cloud-init.service.notes: Main cloud-init service that handles user data processing, SSH key setup, hostname configuration, and user account creation. Essential for cloud instance automation.

code.description: The VS Code Server is a background service that enables remote development with Visual Studio Code. It allows a local VS Code client to connect to a remote machine and edit code, run terminals, and use extensions remotely.
code.notes: May appear as 'code' (code-server by Coder) or as the Microsoft VS Code remote server process. Port varies by configuration.

colord.service.description: Color daemon - System service for managing ICC color profiles and color calibration across input/output devices like monitors, printers, and cameras
colord.service.notes: D-Bus activated daemon for color management. Manages ICC profiles, provides color calibration services, and ensures consistent color reproduction across devices. Used by GNOME and KDE desktop environments.

console-setup.service.description: Console setup service - Configures console fonts, keyboard layout, and character encoding for virtual terminals
console-setup.service.notes: One-shot service that sets up console fonts, keyboard mapping, and character encoding for text-mode terminals. Essential for proper console display and keyboard functionality.

container-getty@1.service.description: A systemd service that provides a virtual terminal (getty) inside a container environment, typically used in systemd-nspawn containers or similar container runtimes to allow interactive login sessions.
container-getty@1.service.notes: Template unit instantiated per container terminal. Analogous to getty@tty1.service but for containers.

containerd.description: Containerd - Industry-standard container runtime that manages container lifecycle, image transfer, and storage with emphasis on simplicity and robustness
containerd.notes: High-level container runtime used by Docker and Kubernetes. Manages container execution, image management, and provides gRPC API for container operations.

containerd.service.description: Containerd daemon - Industry-standard container runtime service that provides container lifecycle management, image operations, and serves as foundation for Docker and Kubernetes
containerd.service.notes: Core container runtime daemon that manages container execution, image management, and storage. Required by Docker and Kubernetes for container operations. CNCF graduated project.

corosync.service.description: Corosync Cluster Engine - Group communication system providing cluster membership, messaging, and quorum services for high availability clusters
corosync.service.notes: Critical clustering service for Proxmox, Pacemaker, and other HA solutions. Provides cluster communication, membership, and consensus. Essential for cluster stability and split-brain prevention.

cron.service.description: Cron daemon - Time-based job scheduler that executes scheduled tasks at specified times and intervals from crontab files
cron.service.notes: Traditional Unix job scheduler daemon. Executes scheduled tasks from system and user crontabs. Essential for automated system maintenance, backups, and periodic tasks.

crond.service.description: crond is the daemon that executes scheduled commands (cron jobs) at specified times and intervals. It reads crontab files from /var/spool/cron and /etc/cron.d to determine what to run.
crond.service.notes: crond is the RHEL/CentOS/Fedora variant; cron is the Debian/Ubuntu variant. Same functionality.

cups-browsed.service.description: CUPS browsed daemon - Automatically discovers network printers via DNS-SD/mDNS and creates local print queues for them
cups-browsed.service.notes: Network printer discovery daemon that automatically creates local CUPS queues for remote printers and IPP devices. Works with Avahi for mDNS printer discovery.

cups.service.description: CUPS printing daemon - Common Unix Printing System that manages print jobs, queues, and provides IPP-based printing services
cups.service.notes: Main printing system daemon providing IPP printing services, job management, and web-based administration. Industry standard for Unix/Linux printing.

cupsd.description: CUPS daemon - The main printing system daemon process that handles print jobs, manages printers, and provides printing services
cupsd.notes: Main CUPS daemon process. Provides IPP printing services, manages print queues, and handles print job processing.

dbus.service.description: D-Bus system message bus - Inter-process communication system that allows applications and system services to communicate securely
dbus.service.notes: Core system service providing secure inter-process communication. Essential for desktop environments, system services, and application communication. Critical system component.

ddclient.service.description: ddclient is a Perl-based client daemon that updates dynamic DNS entries with providers such as Cloudflare, DuckDNS, and many others. It runs as a background service and updates DNS records when the host's IP address changes.

deluge-web.description: The Deluge Web UI is a browser-based interface for the Deluge BitTorrent client. It connects to the deluged daemon and provides remote management of torrents via a web browser.
deluge-web.notes: Default web UI port is 8112. Requires deluged to be running.

deluge-web.service.description: The Deluge Web UI is a browser-based interface for the Deluge BitTorrent client. It connects to the deluged daemon and provides remote management of torrents via a web browser.
deluge-web.service.notes: Systemd service unit for deluge-web. Default port 8112.

deluged.description: deluged is the background daemon process of the Deluge BitTorrent client. It handles all torrent download and upload operations and can be managed via the GTK UI, web UI, or console UI.
deluged.notes: Default daemon port is 58846 (RPC). Torrent ports are typically 6881-6891.

devmgmtd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP device management daemon. Handles device-level management operations and communication within the BIG-IP platform.

devmgmtd.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP device management daemon. Handles device-level management operations and communication within the BIG-IP platform.
devmgmtd.service.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Systemd service variant.

dhclient.service.description: dhclient is the ISC DHCP client daemon that configures network interfaces using DHCP, BOOTP, or static addressing. It requests IP address leases from DHCP servers and renews them as needed.
dhclient.service.notes: Uses UDP port 68 (client) and 67 (server). Configuration at /etc/dhcp/dhclient.conf.

dhclient6.service.description: dhclient running in IPv6 mode, obtaining IPv6 addresses and configuration from DHCPv6 servers. Provides stateful IPv6 address assignment for network interfaces.
dhclient6.service.notes: Uses UDP port 546 (client) and 547 (server) for DHCPv6.

dhcpcd.service.description: dhcpcd is a DHCP and DHCPv6 client daemon that also supports IPv4LL (ZeroConf). It configures network interfaces by obtaining addresses from DHCP servers and is commonly used on Debian and Raspberry Pi systems.
dhcpcd.service.notes: Distinct from ISC dhclient. Supports both IPv4 and IPv6. Default on Raspberry Pi OS.

dm-event.service.access: System service - Device mapper event monitoring
dm-event.service.description: Device mapper event daemon - Monitors device mapper devices for events and executes appropriate actions for LVM and RAID management
dm-event.service.notes: Socket-activated daemon that monitors device mapper devices for failures and other events. Essential for LVM thin provisioning, snapshots, and RAID management.

dnscached.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP DNS caching daemon. Provides DNS caching services within the BIG-IP DNS (GTM) subsystem.

docker-proxy.description: docker-proxy is a userland proxy process spawned by the Docker daemon to forward traffic between the host network and containers when port publishing is used. One instance is created per published port.
docker-proxy.notes: Runs as a child process of dockerd. Can be disabled with --userland-proxy=false in daemon config if kernel supports hairpin NAT.

docker.service.description: Docker daemon - Container platform that provides high-level container management, image building, and orchestration capabilities
docker.service.notes: Popular container platform daemon providing container lifecycle management, image operations, networking, and volumes. Uses containerd as underlying runtime.

dphys-swapfile.service.description: Dynamic swap file service - Automatically creates and manages swap files based on system memory and configuration
dphys-swapfile.service.notes: One-shot service that creates and enables swap files dynamically. Common on Raspberry Pi and embedded systems where swap space needs to be managed automatically.

ecmd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP event command daemon. Handles event processing and command routing within the BIG-IP management plane.

etcd.description: etcd - Distributed, reliable key-value store for the most critical data of a distributed system, commonly used by Kubernetes and other cloud-native applications
etcd.notes: Distributed key-value store providing consistent, fault-tolerant storage for configuration data, service discovery, and coordination. Core component of Kubernetes control plane.

evrouted.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP event routing daemon. Manages event-driven routing and notification within the BIG-IP platform.

expressvpn-daem.description: The ExpressVPN background daemon that manages VPN connections on Linux. It must be running before the ExpressVPN CLI or GUI can connect to a VPN server.
expressvpn-daem.notes: Truncated process name for expressvpn-daemon. Managed via systemctl. Proprietary commercial VPN service.

expressvpn-service.service.description: Systemd service unit for the ExpressVPN background daemon on Linux. Manages VPN tunnel establishment and maintenance for the ExpressVPN commercial VPN service.
expressvpn-service.service.notes: Proprietary commercial VPN service. Supports Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch, and Raspberry Pi OS.

expressvpn.service.description: Systemd service unit for the ExpressVPN background daemon on Linux. Manages VPN tunnel establishment and maintenance for the ExpressVPN commercial VPN service.
expressvpn.service.notes: Proprietary commercial VPN service. Supports Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch, and Raspberry Pi OS.

f5-bootev.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP systemd service that fires boot-time events within the BIG-IP platform initialization sequence.

f5-firewall.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP systemd service for the Advanced Firewall Manager (AFM) subsystem. Manages network firewall policy enforcement.
f5-firewall.service.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal service. Part of the BIG-IP AFM module.

f5-label.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP systemd service for label management within the BIG-IP platform.

f5-module-sysinit.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP systemd service that initializes software modules during system startup. Ensures all licensed BIG-IP modules are properly started.

f5-rest-node.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP Node.js process that serves the iControl REST API. It is the backend for the BIG-IP web UI and REST API consumers.
f5-rest-node.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Runs on port 8100 internally.

f5-swap-eth.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP systemd service that handles Ethernet interface swapping or reassignment during boot, often used in virtual edition deployments.

fake-hwclock.service.description: Fake hardware clock service - Simulates hardware clock functionality on systems without RTC by saving/restoring time from file
fake-hwclock.service.notes: One-shot service for embedded systems without real-time clock. Saves system time to file on shutdown and restores it on boot to maintain time continuity.

finalrd.service.description: Final initrd service - Cleanup service that removes initrd/initramfs files and performs final boot-time housekeeping
finalrd.service.notes: One-shot service that performs cleanup after initial ramdisk transition. Removes temporary boot files and prepares system for normal operation.

fsidd.service.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP filesystem identity daemon. Manages filesystem identity and integrity functions within the BIG-IP platform.

fslogd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP filesystem logging daemon. Handles filesystem-level logging and audit operations within the BIG-IP platform.

fwupd.service.description: fwupd is a system daemon that allows session software and users to update device firmware through the Linux Vendor Firmware Service (LVFS). It supports UEFI capsule updates, Thunderbolt, and many other firmware types.
fwupd.service.notes: Managed via fwupdmgr CLI or GNOME Software. Firmware metadata fetched from LVFS (fwupd.org).

getty@tty1.service.description: getty is a program that manages physical or virtual terminals and prompts for a login. The getty@tty1.service instance manages the first virtual console, providing a text login prompt.
getty@tty1.service.notes: Template unit instantiated per TTY. Part of systemd. Typically runs agetty underneath.

glamor-test.service.description: A one-shot systemd service that tests whether the Glamor OpenGL-based 2D acceleration framework is functional on the system. Used during initialization to probe GPU capabilities.
glamor-test.service.notes: Glamor is an X.Org 2D acceleration library using OpenGL. This service likely runs a capability probe at startup.

gtmd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP Global Traffic Manager daemon. Core process of BIG-IP DNS (formerly GTM) that handles global load balancing decisions across data centers.
gtmd.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Central to BIG-IP DNS functionality.

haproxy.description: HAProxy is a high-performance, open-source TCP/HTTP load balancer and proxy server. It is widely used for distributing traffic across multiple backend servers with health checking, SSL termination, and ACL-based routing.
haproxy.notes: Configuration at /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg. Supports layer 4 and layer 7 load balancing. Default stats port is often 9000.

haproxy.service.description: HAProxy is a high-performance, open-source TCP/HTTP load balancer and proxy server. It is widely used for distributing traffic across multiple backend servers with health checking, SSL termination, and ACL-based routing.
haproxy.service.notes: Systemd service unit for HAProxy. Configuration at /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg.

hddtemp.service.description: hddtemp is a daemon that reads S.M.A.R.T. data from hard drives and reports drive temperatures. When run as a daemon, it listens on a TCP port and returns temperature information on request.
hddtemp.service.notes: Default daemon port is 7634. Requires S.M.A.R.T. support in the drive. Project is largely unmaintained; alternatives include smartmontools.

homarr.service.description: Homarr is a self-hosted, customizable homelab dashboard that provides a unified interface for accessing services, Docker containers, and integrations with tools like Sonarr, Radarr, and more.
homarr.service.notes: Default port is 7575. Supports 40+ integrations and drag-and-drop configuration.

httpd.description: httpd is the Apache HTTP Server daemon, one of the most widely deployed web servers. It serves static and dynamic web content, supports modules for SSL, CGI, reverse proxying, and many other features.
httpd.notes: Configuration at /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf (RHEL) or /etc/apache2/ (Debian). The 'httpd' binary name is used on RHEL/CentOS; Debian uses 'apache2'.

icrd_child.description: Child process of the F5 BIG-IP iControl REST daemon (icrd). Each icrd_child handles REST API requests, and multiple children may run concurrently to serve parallel API calls.
icrd_child.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Memory leaks in icrd_child have been documented in older BIG-IP versions.

ifup@eth0.service.description: A systemd service that brings up the eth0 network interface using the ifup command and /etc/network/interfaces configuration. Part of the ifupdown package on Debian-based systems.
ifup@eth0.service.notes: Template unit for ifupdown-based networking. Debian/Ubuntu specific. May conflict with NetworkManager or systemd-networkd.

ifupdown-pre.service.description: A systemd service that performs pre-configuration tasks before network interfaces are brought up by ifupdown. Ensures the networking environment is ready before interface activation.
ifupdown-pre.service.notes: Debian/Ubuntu specific. Part of the ifupdown network management stack.

ifupdown-wait-online.service.description: A systemd service that waits until all network interfaces configured via ifupdown are online before allowing services that require network connectivity to start.
ifupdown-wait-online.service.notes: Analogous to systemd-networkd-wait-online.service but for ifupdown. Debian/Ubuntu specific.

ifupdown2-pre.service.description: A systemd service for the ifupdown2 network manager that performs pre-configuration tasks before network interfaces are activated. ifupdown2 is a Python rewrite of ifupdown with enhanced capabilities.
ifupdown2-pre.service.notes: Used on Debian, Proxmox VE, and Cumulus Linux. ifupdown2 supports VLAN-aware bridges and VRF.

iperf3.description: iperf3 is a tool for performing active network bandwidth measurements between two endpoints. When run in server mode (-s), it listens for incoming test connections and reports throughput, latency, and packet loss.
iperf3.notes: Default port is 5201. Supports TCP, UDP, and SCTP. Developed by ESnet/Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

iperf3.service.description: iperf3 is a tool for performing active network bandwidth measurements between two endpoints. When run in server mode (-s), it listens for incoming test connections and reports throughput, latency, and packet loss.
iperf3.service.notes: Systemd service unit for iperf3 in persistent server mode. Default port is 5201.

ipvsadm.service.description: ipvsadm is a user-space utility for managing the IP Virtual Server (IPVS) kernel subsystem, which provides layer-4 load balancing. This systemd service loads and restores saved IPVS rules at boot.
ipvsadm.service.notes: IPVS is used by Kubernetes kube-proxy in IPVS mode. Rules are kernel-level; ipvsadm is just the management tool.

irqbalance.service.description: irqbalance is a daemon that distributes hardware interrupt requests (IRQs) across CPU cores to improve system performance. It identifies high-volume interrupt sources and spreads the load to minimize cache misses.
irqbalance.service.notes: Important on multi-core systems. May be disabled on single-core or real-time systems. Configuration at /etc/sysconfig/irqbalance.

iscsid.service.description: iscsid implements the control path of the iSCSI protocol for the open-iSCSI initiator. It manages iSCSI sessions, handles login and authentication, and coordinates with the kernel iSCSI driver.
iscsid.service.notes: Default iSCSI port is 3260. Configuration at /etc/iscsi/iscsid.conf. Managed with iscsiadm CLI tool.

jackett.description: Jackett is a proxy server that translates queries from applications like Sonarr and Radarr into tracker-specific HTTP queries. It supports hundreds of public and private torrent trackers as indexers.
jackett.notes: Default port is 9117. Largely superseded by Prowlarr for *arr integrations. Primary documentation is the GitHub README.

jackett.service.description: Jackett is a proxy server that translates queries from applications like Sonarr and Radarr into tracker-specific HTTP queries. It supports hundreds of public and private torrent trackers as indexers.
jackett.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Jackett. Default port is 9117.

java.description: Not a standalone service; this is the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) runtime process. It appears in process listings when any Java-based application is running.
java.notes: Identify the parent application using 'ps aux | grep java' or 'jps' to determine which Java application is running.

jellyfin.description: Jellyfin is a free and open-source media server that allows you to manage and stream movies, TV shows, music, and photos. It is a fork of Emby and provides client apps for all major platforms.
jellyfin.notes: Default HTTP port is 8096, HTTPS is 8920. No premium license required unlike Plex/Emby.

jellyfin.service.description: Jellyfin is a free and open-source media server that allows you to manage and stream movies, TV shows, music, and photos. It is a fork of Emby and provides client apps for all major platforms.
jellyfin.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Jellyfin. Default HTTP port is 8096.

jellyseerr.service.description: Jellyseerr is an open-source media request and discovery manager for Jellyfin (forked from Overseerr). It allows users to browse and request movies and TV shows, which are then sent to Radarr or Sonarr for download.
jellyseerr.service.notes: Default port is 5055. Transitioning to 'Seerr' project with docs at docs.seerr.dev.

kerneloops.service.description: kerneloops is a daemon that monitors system logs for Linux kernel oops and panic messages, collects the crash signatures, and optionally submits them to oops.kernel.org for statistical analysis by kernel developers.
kerneloops.service.notes: Configuration at /etc/kerneloops.conf. Requires user permission before submitting data. Primarily used on Ubuntu desktops.

keyboard-setup.service.description: A systemd one-shot service that configures keyboard layout and console font settings at boot using the configuration in /etc/default/keyboard and /etc/default/console-setup.
keyboard-setup.service.notes: Debian/Ubuntu specific. Runs setupcon to apply keyboard layout configuration.

keymgmtd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP key management daemon. Handles cryptographic key management, certificate operations, and SSL/TLS key storage within the BIG-IP platform.

kmod-static-nodes.service.description: A systemd service that creates static device nodes in /dev for kernel modules that need them before the modules are loaded. Ensures device nodes exist for modules like DRM and sound.
kmod-static-nodes.service.notes: Runs early in boot before udev. Uses kmod to generate static device nodes.

ksmtuned.service.description: ksmtuned is a script-based daemon that dynamically tunes Kernel Samepage Merging (KSM), which allows the kernel to de-duplicate identical memory pages across virtual machines. It adjusts KSM aggressiveness based on available memory.
ksmtuned.service.notes: Configuration at /etc/ksmtuned.conf. KSM provides memory savings in virtualization environments like KVM/Proxmox.

kube-apiserver.description: kube-apiserver is the central management component of Kubernetes that exposes the Kubernetes API. All cluster components communicate through it, and it validates and stores cluster state in etcd.
kube-apiserver.notes: Default secure port is 6443. The single point of truth for all cluster state. Scales horizontally.

kube-controller.description: kube-controller-manager runs the core Kubernetes controllers (node, replication, endpoints, service account, etc.) that watch cluster state and make changes to drive toward the desired state.
kube-controller.notes: Truncated process name for kube-controller-manager. Default secure port is 10257.

kube-proxy.description: kube-proxy is a network proxy that runs on each Kubernetes node, maintaining network rules that allow communication to Pods. It implements part of the Kubernetes Service concept using iptables, IPVS, or userspace modes.
kube-proxy.notes: Default health check port is 10256. Mode (iptables vs IPVS) affects performance at scale.

kube-rbac-proxy.description: kube-rbac-proxy is a small HTTP proxy that enforces Kubernetes RBAC authorization for a single upstream service. It is commonly used as a sidecar to protect metrics endpoints and other internal services.
kube-rbac-proxy.notes: Commonly deployed alongside Prometheus exporters to protect /metrics endpoints. Primary documentation is the GitHub README.

kube-scheduler.description: kube-scheduler watches for newly created Pods with no assigned node and selects a suitable node for them to run on, based on resource requirements, constraints, affinity rules, and other policies.
kube-scheduler.notes: Default secure port is 10259. Pluggable scheduler framework allows custom scheduling algorithms.

kubectl.description: kubectl is the command-line tool for interacting with Kubernetes clusters. It is not a daemon but a CLI utility used to deploy, inspect, and manage cluster resources.
kubectl.notes: CLI tool, not a daemon or service. If appearing in process list, it is a running command-line invocation.

kubelet.description: kubelet is the primary node agent that runs on every Kubernetes worker node. It ensures that containers described in PodSpecs are running and healthy, and communicates node status to the control plane.
kubelet.notes: Default port is 10250. Runs on every node in the cluster including control plane nodes.

kubelet.service.description: kubelet is the primary node agent that runs on every Kubernetes worker node. It ensures that containers described in PodSpecs are running and healthy, and communicates node status to the control plane.
kubelet.service.notes: Systemd service unit for kubelet. Default port is 10250.

ldconfig.service.description: A systemd one-shot service that runs ldconfig to rebuild the cache of shared library locations. It ensures applications can find dynamically linked libraries after packages are installed or updated.
ldconfig.service.notes: Runs ldconfig to update /etc/ld.so.cache. Typically triggered after package installation by package managers.

lidarr.service.description: Lidarr is an automatic music collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users that monitors RSS feeds for new albums and manages downloads. It integrates with download clients and media servers to organize and rename music files.
lidarr.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Lidarr. Default web UI port is 8686.

lightdm.service.description: LightDM is a lightweight, cross-desktop display manager that provides the graphical login screen and manages X11 display sessions. It runs greeters to allow users to select user accounts and session types.

lm-sensors.service.description: Hardware monitoring sensors initialization service - loads kernel modules and sets up hardware sensors for monitoring CPU temperature, voltage, fan speeds, and other hardware parameters
lm-sensors.service.notes: Provides userspace support for hardware monitoring drivers. Monitors CPU temperature, voltage, fan speeds. Requires sensors-detect to be run first to identify available sensors.

lvm2-monitor.service.description: Monitoring of LVM2 mirrors, snapshots and other logical volumes using dmeventd or progress polling for automatic repair and management
lvm2-monitor.service.notes: Essential for automatic repair of LVM mirrors and snapshots. Monitors LVM volumes for failures and triggers automatic recovery processes.

lxc-monitord.service.description: LXC container monitoring daemon - monitors state of Linux containers and provides monitoring services for container lifecycle events
lxc-monitord.service.notes: Monitors container state changes and provides interface for container monitoring. Waits 30 seconds for new clients before exiting when no clients are connected.

lxc-net.service.description: LXC network bridge setup service - configures network bridges and networking infrastructure for Linux containers
lxc-net.service.notes: Sets up network bridges for container networking. Creates lxcbr0 bridge interface for container communication.

lxc.service.description: LXC container service - manages Linux containers startup and autostart functionality for system containers
lxc.service.notes: Main LXC service for container management. Handles container autostart and system container operations.

lxcfs.service.description: LXCFS - FUSE filesystem for containers that virtualizes /proc and /sys to provide container-aware resource information
lxcfs.service.notes: Provides container-aware view of system resources by virtualizing /proc files like cpuinfo, meminfo, stat, uptime. Makes containers see only their allocated resources.

mariadb.service.description: MariaDB database server - open source relational database management system, MySQL-compatible SQL database server
mariadb.service.notes: High-performance MySQL-compatible database server. Default port 3306. Supports SQL, replication, and clustering.

mariadbd.description: MariaDB database daemon process - the actual database server process that handles SQL queries and database operations
mariadbd.notes: The main MariaDB server daemon process. Runs as part of mariadb.service.

master.description: Postfix MTA master daemon — manages all Postfix service processes (smtp, smtpd, cleanup, qmgr, etc.)
master.notes: Confirmed Postfix on labinator (/usr/lib/postfix/sbin/master -w). Present on 24 hosts — Postfix is installed by default on many Debian/Ubuntu systems for local mail delivery.

mcpd.description: mcpd (Master Control Program Daemon) is the central configuration management process of F5 BIG-IP. All configuration changes pass through mcpd, which then distributes them to other BIG-IP processes.
mcpd.display_name: F5 BIG-IP Master Control Program Daemon
mcpd.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP core process. Restarting mcpd causes a brief interruption in traffic processing.

miniserv.pl.description: Webmin miniserv daemon - lightweight web server that provides web-based system administration interface for Unix systems
miniserv.pl.notes: Webmin's built-in web server written in Perl. Provides secure web-based administration interface typically on port 10000.

mongod.description: mongod is the primary daemon process of MongoDB, a document-oriented NoSQL database. It handles data requests, manages data access, and performs background management operations.
mongod.notes: Default port is 27017. Configuration at /etc/mongod.conf. Shard members use 27018, config servers use 27019.

mongod.service.description: mongod is the primary daemon process of MongoDB, a document-oriented NoSQL database. It handles data requests, manages data access, and performs background management operations.
mongod.service.notes: Systemd service unit for MongoDB. Default port is 27017.

monpd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP health monitor daemon. Performs active health checks against pool members and updates their availability status in real time.
monpd.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Core to BIG-IP's load balancing health monitoring.

mosquitto.description: Eclipse Mosquitto is an open-source MQTT message broker that implements MQTT protocol versions 5.0, 3.1.1, and 3.1. It is lightweight and suitable for IoT and home automation messaging between devices.
mosquitto.notes: Default plain-text port is 1883; TLS port is 8883. WebSocket support on 9001. Widely used with Home Assistant.

mosquitto.service.description: Eclipse Mosquitto is an open-source MQTT message broker that implements MQTT protocol versions 5.0, 3.1.1, and 3.1. It is lightweight and suitable for IoT and home automation messaging between devices.
mosquitto.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Mosquitto. Default plain-text port is 1883.

multipathd.service.description: Device mapper multipath daemon - manages multiple I/O paths to storage devices for redundancy and performance
multipathd.service.notes: Manages multiple paths to storage devices (SAN storage). Provides failover and load balancing for storage I/O paths.

mysqld.description: mysqld is the MySQL database server daemon. It is a multi-threaded SQL database management system that handles all database operations, queries, and connections.
mysqld.notes: Default port is 3306. Configuration at /etc/mysql/my.cnf or /etc/my.cnf. MariaDB uses the same process name.

named.description: BIND DNS server daemon process - the actual DNS server process that handles domain name resolution queries
named.notes: The main BIND DNS server daemon process. Runs as part of named.service.

named.service.description: BIND Domain Name Server - authoritative and recursive DNS server for domain name resolution (also known as bind9)
named.service.notes: Full-featured DNS server supporting authoritative and recursive queries, DNSSEC, zone transfers. Uses ports 53 (UDP/TCP) and 953 (rndc control).

networkd-dispatcher.service.description: Networkd dispatcher service - executes scripts in response to network state changes managed by systemd-networkd
networkd-dispatcher.service.notes: Runs custom scripts when network interfaces change state. Integrates with systemd-networkd for network event handling.

networking.service.description: Network interface configuration service - brings up network interfaces using ifupdown scripts and /etc/network/interfaces
networking.service.notes: Traditional Debian/Ubuntu network configuration service. Configures network interfaces based on /etc/network/interfaces file.

nfs-blkmap.service.description: A systemd service for the NFS pNFS (parallel NFS) block layout driver. It maps block device layouts for pNFS clients, enabling direct block-level storage access in pNFS deployments.
nfs-blkmap.service.notes: Only relevant when using pNFS block layout. Not needed for standard NFSv4 deployments.

nfs-kernel-server.service.description: The NFS kernel server provides Network File System (NFS) file sharing from the Linux kernel. It allows remote clients to mount and access shared directories over the network.
nfs-kernel-server.service.notes: Default NFS port is 2049, RPC portmapper is 111. Exports configured in /etc/exports. Requires rpcbind and optionally nfs-blkmap.

nftables.service.description: Netfilter Tables firewall service - modern Linux firewall framework that replaces iptables for packet filtering and network security
nftables.service.notes: Modern replacement for iptables. Provides packet filtering, NAT, and network security rules. Uses single kernel module with improved syntax.

nginx.description: Nginx is a high-performance web server and reverse proxy server. It is widely used for serving static content, load balancing, HTTP caching, and as a TLS termination point.
nginx.notes: Configuration at /etc/nginx/nginx.conf. Known for low memory footprint and high concurrency. Also used as a reverse proxy for many self-hosted services.

nginx.service.command_line: nginx: master process /usr/sbin/nginx -g daemon on; master_process on;
nginx.service.description: Nginx is a high-performance web server and reverse proxy server. It is widely used for serving static content, load balancing, HTTP caching, and as a TLS termination point.
nginx.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Nginx. Configuration at /etc/nginx/nginx.conf.

nlad.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP network layer access daemon. Handles network layer access policy enforcement, typically associated with BIG-IP APM network access tunnels.

nmbd.service.description: Samba NetBIOS Name Server daemon - provides NetBIOS name resolution and browsing services for Windows network neighborhood
nmbd.service.notes: Handles NetBIOS name resolution and Windows network browsing. Uses ports 137 (UDP name service) and 138 (UDP datagram service). Can act as WINS server.

node.description: Not a standalone service; this is the Node.js JavaScript runtime process. It appears in process listings when any Node.js application is running.
node.notes: Identify the specific application by examining command-line arguments with 'ps aux | grep node'.

node_exporter.description: Prometheus Node Exporter - system metrics collector that exports hardware and kernel-related metrics for monitoring CPU, memory, disk, network, and other system statistics
node_exporter.notes: Exports system metrics on port 9100 by default. Collects CPU usage, memory consumption, disk I/O, network statistics, and other hardware metrics for Prometheus monitoring.

nsolid.description: N|Solid is an enhanced Node.js runtime by NodeSource that provides production monitoring, security scanning, and performance profiling with no code changes required. It is a drop-in replacement for Node.js.
nsolid.notes: Commercially supported Node.js runtime. Includes the N|Solid Agent for metrics collection.

ntp.service.description: Network Time Protocol daemon - synchronizes system clock with remote NTP servers to maintain accurate time across networks
ntp.service.notes: Provides accurate time synchronization using UDP port 123. Essential for distributed systems, security mechanisms, and coordinated operations.

octoprint.description: OctoPrint is a web interface for 3D printers that provides remote monitoring and control, including a webcam feed, temperature graphs, print progress tracking, and plugin support. Typically runs on a Raspberry Pi connected to the printer.
octoprint.notes: Default port is 5000. Highly extensible via plugins at plugins.octoprint.org.

ollama.description: Ollama is a tool for running large language models (LLMs) locally. It provides a server with an OpenAI-compatible REST API, enabling local AI inference with models like Llama, Mistral, Gemma, and many others.
ollama.notes: Default API port is 11434. Supports GPU acceleration. Models stored in ~/.ollama/models.

omapd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP object map daemon. Manages object mapping and lookup services within the BIG-IP platform configuration database.

openclaw-gatewa.description: OpenClaw Gateway is the always-on routing and control plane process for the OpenClaw personal AI assistant platform. It manages channel connections (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, etc.) and routes messages to AI backends.
openclaw-gatewa.notes: Truncated process name for openclaw-gateway. Default port is 18789 (localhost only). Installed as a systemd user service.

openclaw.service.description: The OpenClaw systemd service runs the OpenClaw personal AI assistant platform, which routes AI requests across multiple messaging channels. It installs as a systemd service on Linux via the OpenClaw onboarding wizard.
openclaw.service.notes: Personal AI assistant with multi-channel support. Default API port is 18789.

pabnagd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP policy agent daemon. Part of the policy enforcement subsystem within BIG-IP APM or ASM modules.

packagekit.service.description: PackageKit daemon - provides D-Bus abstraction layer for package management systems, enabling unified package installation, removal, and updates across different distributions
packagekit.service.notes: System-activated daemon that abstracts package management operations. Provides unified API for package operations across different Linux distributions via D-Bus.

patchmon-agent.service.description: A custom patch monitoring agent service that collects patch/update status from systems and reports to a central server. Not a known standard open-source project.
patchmon-agent.service.notes: Likely a custom/private patch monitoring solution. Inspect the systemd unit file for configuration details.

patchmon-server.service.description: A custom patch monitoring server service that aggregates patch/update status from agent nodes. Not a known standard open-source project.
patchmon-server.service.notes: Likely a custom/private patch monitoring solution. Inspect the systemd unit file for configuration details.

pbs-network-config-commit.service.description: A Proxmox Backup Server systemd service that applies pending network configuration changes at boot. It ensures any network changes staged through the PBS web UI are committed when the system starts.
pbs-network-config-commit.service.display_name: Proxmox Backup Server Network Config Commit
pbs-network-config-commit.service.notes: Part of Proxmox Backup Server. Analogous to the network config commit service in Proxmox VE.

pcp-reboot-init.service.description: A Performance Co-Pilot (PCP) systemd service that performs initialization tasks related to post-reboot state cleanup for PCP performance data archives and logging infrastructure.
pcp-reboot-init.service.notes: Part of the PCP toolkit. Enabled alongside pmcd and pmlogger.

perl.description: Perl interpreter process - dynamic programming language commonly used for system administration, web development, and text processing scripts
perl.notes: Perl interpreter running as a process. Commonly used for system scripts, text processing, and web applications. May be running Webmin's miniserv.pl or other Perl-based services.

pihole-FTL.description: Pi-hole FTL (Faster Than Light) daemon process - the main Pi-hole DNS filtering and blocking engine that provides network-wide ad blocking
pihole-FTL.notes: Core Pi-hole process that handles DNS queries and ad blocking. Runs as part of pihole-FTL.service.

pihole-FTL.service.description: Pi-hole FTL DNS service - network-wide ad and tracker blocking DNS server with web interface for management and statistics
pihole-FTL.service.notes: Network-level ad blocking DNS server. Blocks ads, trackers, and malicious domains for all devices on the network. Includes web interface on ports 80/443.

plymouth-quit-wait.service.description: Plymouth boot splash quit wait service - waits for Plymouth boot splash screen to complete before proceeding with other boot services
plymouth-quit-wait.service.notes: Part of Plymouth boot splash system. Ensures proper shutdown of boot splash screen during system startup.

plymouth-quit.service.description: Plymouth boot splash quit service - terminates the Plymouth boot splash screen when system boot is complete
plymouth-quit.service.notes: Terminates Plymouth boot splash screen. Part of the graphical boot process that provides visual feedback during system startup.

plymouth-read-write.service.description: Plymouth read-write service - ensures filesystem is ready for Plymouth boot splash operations during system startup
plymouth-read-write.service.notes: Prepares filesystem for Plymouth operations. Ensures read-write access is available for boot splash functionality.

pm2-root.service.description: PM2 is a production process manager for Node.js applications with a built-in load balancer. The pm2-root.service is the systemd startup service for PM2 running under the root user account.
pm2-root.service.notes: Manages Node.js application processes. The 'root' suffix indicates the service is for the root user's PM2 instance.

pmcd.description: pmcd (Performance Metrics Collection Daemon) is the central coordinator of the Performance Co-Pilot (PCP) toolkit. It accepts connections from PCP client tools and routes metric requests to the appropriate Performance Metrics Domain Agents (PMDAs).
pmcd.notes: Default port is 44321. Configuration at /etc/pcp/pmcd/pmcd.conf. Part of the PCP performance monitoring toolkit.

pmcd.service.description: pmcd (Performance Metrics Collection Daemon) is the central coordinator of the Performance Co-Pilot (PCP) toolkit. It accepts connections from PCP client tools and routes metric requests to the appropriate Performance Metrics Domain Agents (PMDAs).
pmcd.service.notes: Systemd service unit for pmcd. Default port is 44321.

pmlogger.description: pmlogger is a PCP tool that continuously archives performance metric values from pmcd into log files. These archives can later be replayed and analyzed with PCP client tools.
pmlogger.notes: Default port is 44322. Archives stored in /var/log/pcp/pmlogger/. Part of the PCP toolkit.

pmlogger.service.description: pmlogger is a PCP tool that continuously archives performance metric values from pmcd into log files. These archives can later be replayed and analyzed with PCP client tools.

pmlogger_farm.service.description: A PCP (Performance Co-Pilot) service that manages multiple pmlogger instances for logging metrics from multiple hosts. Used in environments where a central host collects performance data from many systems.
pmlogger_farm.service.notes: Part of the PCP toolkit for centralized multi-host metric collection.

polkit.service.description: PolicyKit authorization service - provides framework for granting unprivileged processes access to privileged operations through policy-based authorization
polkit.service.notes: Authorization framework for privileged operations. Often called 'the sudo of systemd'. Manages authentication dialogs and privilege escalation.

postfix.service.description: Postfix Mail Transport Agent - SMTP mail server for sending, receiving, and relaying email messages between mail servers and clients
postfix.service.notes: High-performance mail server. Uses ports 25 (SMTP), 465 (SMTPS), and 587 (submission). Supports TLS encryption and SASL authentication.

postfix@-.service.access: Mail service instance - configurable network access
postfix@-.service.description: Postfix Mail Transport Agent instance - individual Postfix instance for multi-instance mail server configurations
postfix@-.service.notes: Postfix instance service for multi-instance configurations. Allows running multiple independent Postfix instances on the same server.

postgres.description: PostgreSQL is a powerful, open-source object-relational database system. The 'postgres' process is the primary server daemon that manages database files, connections, and query processing.

prowlarr.service.description: Prowlarr is an indexer manager and proxy for the *arr application stack, supporting both torrent trackers and Usenet indexers. It centralizes indexer management for Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr, and similar applications.
prowlarr.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Prowlarr. Default port is 9696.

proxmox-backup-.description: Truncated process name for one of the Proxmox Backup Server services (proxmox-backup.service or proxmox-backup-proxy.service). Proxmox Backup Server is an enterprise backup solution for VMs, containers, and physical hosts.
proxmox-backup-.notes: Truncated process name. The full service is likely proxmox-backup.service (API server) or proxmox-backup-proxy.service (HTTPS proxy). Default port is 8007.

proxmox-backup-banner.service.description: A Proxmox Backup Server systemd service that displays the system banner (version info, IP addresses) on the console at boot. Provides a quick reference for administrators.
proxmox-backup-banner.service.notes: Cosmetic service that updates /etc/issue with system information after network is up.

proxmox-backup-proxy.service.description: The Proxmox Backup Server HTTPS proxy service that handles TLS termination and routes API requests to the backend proxmox-backup.service. It serves the PBS web UI on port 8007.
proxmox-backup-proxy.service.notes: Default port is 8007. Handles SSL certificates for the PBS web interface.

proxmox-backup.service.description: The main Proxmox Backup Server API service. Handles backup operations, datastore management, and all API requests for the PBS platform.
proxmox-backup.service.notes: Backend API service; the proxy (proxmox-backup-proxy.service) handles external HTTPS access on port 8007.

racoon.description: racoon is an IKE (Internet Key Exchange) daemon from the KAME project used to automatically negotiate and manage IPsec security associations. It implements IKEv1 for establishing encrypted tunnels.
racoon.notes: Uses UDP 500 (IKE) and 4500 (NAT-T). Legacy software; largely replaced by strongSwan or Libreswan. Part of ipsec-tools package.

radarr.service.description: Radarr is an automatic movie collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users that monitors for new movies and manages downloads. It integrates with download clients and media servers to organize and rename movie files.
radarr.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Radarr. Default port is 7878.

readarr.service.description: Readarr is an ebook and audiobook collection manager for Usenet and BitTorrent users, similar to Sonarr but for books. Note: the project was officially retired in early 2026 due to metadata source issues.
readarr.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Readarr. Project is retired/unmaintained as of 2026. Default port is 8787.

redis-server.description: Redis is an open-source, in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, message broker, and streaming engine. It supports strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, and more.
redis-server.notes: Default port is 6379. Configuration at /etc/redis/redis.conf. Persistence is optional (RDB/AOF).

redis-server.service.description: Redis is an open-source, in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, message broker, and streaming engine. It supports strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, and more.
redis-server.service.notes: Systemd service unit for Redis. Default port is 6379.

rpc-statd-notify.service.description: NFS status notification service - notifies NFS peers about status changes after system restart for proper lock recovery
rpc-statd-notify.service.notes: Notifies NFS clients and servers about status changes. Essential for NFS lock recovery and state management.

rpc.mountd.description: rpc.mountd is the NFS server-side mount daemon that implements the NFS MOUNT protocol. It handles client mount requests (NFS v2/v3) and verifies client access against /etc/exports.
rpc.mountd.notes: Uses a dynamic RPC port registered with rpcbind. Required for NFSv2 and NFSv3. NFSv4 does not use mountd.

rpc.statd.description: NFS status monitor daemon - monitors NFS file locks and handles crash recovery notifications between NFS clients and servers
rpc.statd.notes: Monitors NFS lock status and provides crash recovery. Works with rpc-statd-notify.service for proper NFS state management.

rpcbind.description: RPC port mapper service - maps RPC program numbers to network ports, essential for NFS and other RPC-based services
rpcbind.notes: Universal addresses to RPC program number mapper. Required for NFS, NIS, and other RPC services. Uses port 111.

rpi-eeprom-update.service.description: Raspberry Pi EEPROM update service - automatically checks for and applies bootloader and VL805 USB controller firmware updates
rpi-eeprom-update.service.notes: Raspberry Pi specific service for updating bootloader firmware. Ensures the Pi has the latest stable firmware for optimal performance and compatibility.

rsync.description: rsync is a fast, versatile file synchronization and transfer tool that uses a delta-transfer algorithm to minimize data transfer. When appearing as a running process, it may be performing a scheduled backup or running in daemon mode for remote sync.
rsync.notes: Default rsync daemon port is 873. Daemon mode configured via /etc/rsyncd.conf. Commonly used for backups.

rsyslog.service.description: System logging daemon - collects, processes, and stores system log messages from kernel, applications, and services
rsyslog.service.notes: Enhanced syslogd with advanced filtering, forwarding, and output capabilities. Supports remote logging via port 514 (UDP/TCP).

setvtrgb.service.description: Virtual terminal RGB color service - sets default color palette for virtual console terminals during system startup
setvtrgb.service.notes: Configures color palette for virtual terminals. Ensures consistent terminal colors across different console sessions.

smbd.description: Samba file sharing daemon process - provides SMB/CIFS file and printer sharing services for Windows and Linux clients
smbd.notes: Main Samba daemon for file and printer sharing. Uses ports 139 and 445 for SMB/CIFS protocol. Runs as part of smbd.service.

snapd.seeded.service.description: A systemd service that indicates the snapd daemon has completed its initial seeding phase (pre-installing seed snaps from the installation media). It is a one-time milestone service.
snapd.seeded.service.notes: Part of the snapd (Snap daemon) ecosystem. Runs once after initial snap seeding is complete.

snmpd.description: snmpd is the Net-SNMP agent daemon that responds to SNMP queries for management information about the local system, including CPU, memory, disk, network interfaces, and custom extensions.
snmpd.notes: Default UDP port is 161 (queries) and 162 (traps). Configuration at /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf.

snmpd.service.description: Simple Network Management Protocol daemon - provides network monitoring and management capabilities through SNMP protocol
snmpd.service.notes: Network monitoring agent that responds to SNMP queries on port 161. Provides system information, network statistics, and device monitoring data.

sod.description: The F5 BIG-IP System Operations Daemon (sod) is responsible for high availability failover operations. It monitors system health and triggers failover between active and standby BIG-IP units.
sod.notes: Proprietary F5 BIG-IP internal process. Critical for HA failover functionality.

speaker.description: System audio/speaker service for managing sound output and audio device control
speaker.notes: Generic audio service for system speaker control and sound management. May be related to ALSA, PulseAudio, or other audio subsystems.

ssh.service.description: OpenSSH is the standard SSH server that provides encrypted remote login and command execution. It allows secure remote administration and file transfers via the SSH protocol.
ssh.service.notes: Default port is 22. Configuration at /etc/ssh/sshd_config. Service name is 'ssh' on Debian, 'sshd' on RHEL/CentOS.

sshd.description: OpenSSH daemon providing secure encrypted remote shell access and file transfer capabilities
sshd.notes: Secure Shell daemon that listens for connections from SSH clients. Provides encrypted communication for remote login, command execution, and file transfer.

stash-linux.description: Stash is an open-source self-hosted adult media organizer written in Go. It scans, organizes, and manages adult video libraries with support for metadata scraping from online sources.
stash-linux.notes: Default port is 9999. The 'stash-linux' binary is the Linux executable. Documentation at docs.stashapp.cc.

stats.pl.description: Perl script for collecting and analyzing system statistics, performance metrics, or log data
stats.pl.notes: Custom Perl script for statistics collection. Could be related to system monitoring, web analytics, network traffic analysis, or performance monitoring. Common in server monitoring setups.

steam.description: Steam digital distribution platform client for gaming, game management, and social networking
steam.notes: Valve's Steam gaming platform client providing game library management, digital rights management, multiplayer gaming, and social features. Developed by Valve Corporation.

sysstat.service.description: sysstat is a collection of performance monitoring tools for Linux including sar, iostat, mpstat, and pidstat. The sysstat.service collects and saves system activity data to disk for historical analysis.
sysstat.service.notes: Data stored in /var/log/sysstat/ or /var/log/sa/. Use 'sar' to query historical data.

systemd.description: System and service manager that runs as PID 1, providing aggressive parallelization capabilities, socket activation, and dependency-based service control
systemd.notes: Core system initialization and service management suite for Linux. Provides system logging, mount point management, device management, and process supervision. Replacement for SysV init.

tailscaled.description: tailscaled is the background daemon for Tailscale, a zero-config WireGuard-based VPN that creates a secure mesh network between devices. It manages peer connections, key exchange, and routing.
tailscaled.notes: Default UDP port is 41641 (but uses DERP relay servers when direct connections fail). Managed via 'tailscale' CLI.

tmipsecd.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP IPsec daemon that manages IPsec tunnel establishment, maintenance, and key management for BIG-IP IPsec VPN functionality.

tmrouted.description: Internal F5 BIG-IP routing daemon that manages dynamic routing protocols and route table maintenance within the BIG-IP platform.

tvheadend.description: TVHeadend is an open-source TV streaming server for Linux that supports ATSC, DVB-C/C2, DVB-S/S2, DVB-T/T2, IPTV, and SAT>IP input sources. It provides EPG, recording, and transcoding capabilities.
tvheadend.notes: Default HTTP port is 9981, HTSP (streaming) port is 9982. Commonly used with Kodi and other HTSP clients.

uptime-kuma.description: Uptime Kuma is a self-hosted monitoring tool with a fancy web UI for tracking the uptime of websites, services, TCP ports, and more. It supports notifications via Telegram, Slack, email, and many other channels.
uptime-kuma.notes: Default port is 3001. Supports HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, DNS, and ping monitors. Notification integrations are extensive.

vncserver-x11-c.description: VNC server providing remote desktop access to X11 display sessions over network connections
vncserver-x11-c.notes: Virtual Network Computing server enabling remote desktop access to existing X11 sessions. Allows viewing and controlling desktop environments from remote locations. Security configuration essential.
